q.en: Where and how was Booker T. Washington born?
q.zh: Booker T. Washington 出生在何处、处于什么境遇？

options.en: He was born a slave on a plantation in Virginia.
options.zh: 他出生在弗吉尼亚州的一个种植园里，是个奴隶。

options.en: He was born free in a big city in the North.
options.zh: 他出生在北方一座大城市的自由人家庭。

options.en: He was born in Africa and brought to America.
options.zh: 他出生在非洲，后来被带到美国。

options.en: He was born to a wealthy plantation owner.
options.zh: 他出生在一个富有的种植园主家庭。

explain.en: The introduction and 'Home Life' section state that Booker T. Washington was born a slave on a plantation in Virginia and was not sure of the exact place or date.
explain.zh: 引言和'Home Life'一节说明 Booker T. Washington 出生在弗吉尼亚州一个种植园里，是个奴隶，连确切的地点和日期都不清楚。

q.en: What was the worst piece of clothing Booker had to wear as a slave?
q.zh: 作为奴隶，Booker 不得不穿的最糟糕的衣服是什么？

options.en: A silk suit given to him by the master.
options.zh: 主人送给他的丝绸西装。

options.en: A warm wool coat.
options.zh: 一件暖和的羊毛大衣。

options.en: A flax shirt made from rough leftover cloth that felt like pinpoints in his flesh.
options.zh: 用粗糙的边角亚麻布做的亚麻衬衫，穿在身上像无数针刺进皮肉。

options.en: Leather boots.
options.zh: 皮靴。

explain.en: The book says the worst thing he wore was a flax shirt made from the cheapest, roughest leftover cloth, which felt 'like a hundred small pinpoints' in his flesh.
explain.zh: 书中说最难受的是亚麻衬衫，用最便宜、最粗糙的边角布做成，穿在身上像'一百根小针'刺进皮肉。

q.en: What job did young Booker dread doing as a slave boy?
q.zh: 作为奴隶男孩，小 Booker 最害怕做的是什么活？

options.en: Reading books to the master's children.
options.zh: 给主人的孩子读书。

options.en: Carrying corn to the mill about three miles away, where the heavy bag often fell off the horse.
options.zh: 把玉米运到约三英里外的磨坊，沉重的袋子经常从马上掉下来。

options.en: Sailing a boat on the river.
options.zh: 在河上划船。

options.en: Building the plantation house.
options.zh: 建造种植园主屋。

explain.en: In 'The Work of a Slave Boy,' Booker says he took corn to the mill once a week, about three miles away, and dreaded it because the bag kept shifting and falling off the horse.
explain.zh: 在'The Work of a Slave Boy'一节，Booker 说他每周把玉米运到约三英里外的磨坊，很害怕因为袋子总挪动并从马上掉下来。

q.en: How did the slaves first learn that they were slaves?
q.zh: 奴隶们最初是如何意识到自己是奴隶的？

options.en: When his mother knelt praying for Abraham Lincoln and the Northern armies to free them.
options.zh: 当他的母亲跪下祈祷，希望亚伯拉罕·林肯和北方军队让他们获得自由时。

options.en: When they were sent to school.
options.zh: 当他们被送去上学时。

options.en: When they read a newspaper.
options.zh: 当他们读到报纸时。

options.en: When the master told them directly.
options.zh: 当主人直接告诉他们时。

explain.en: In 'Talk of Freedom,' Booker says the first time he knew they were slaves was when his mother woke them early to pray that Abraham Lincoln and the Northern armies might succeed and they might be free.
explain.zh: 在'Talk of Freedom'一节，Booker 说第一次知道自己身为奴隶，是母亲清晨叫醒他们，祈祷林肯和北方军队胜利、他们能获自由。

q.en: How did the enslaved people feel toward the white people, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，被奴役的人对白人是什么感受？

options.en: They hated all white people.
options.zh: 他们憎恨所有白人。

options.en: They wanted to fight the master's family.
options.zh: 他们想和主人一家打斗。

options.en: They ignored the white family completely.
options.zh: 他们完全无视白人一家。

options.en: They were not bitter; they felt sorrow when a young white owner was killed.
options.zh: 他们并不怨恨；当一位年轻的白人主人阵亡时，他们感到悲伤。

explain.en: The book says the slaves were not bitter; when a young white owner (Master Billy) was killed, the slaves felt sorrow because some had cared for him as a baby and played with him as a child.
explain.zh: 书中说奴隶们并不怨恨；当年轻的白人主人（Billy 少爷）阵亡时，他们感到悲伤，因为有些人曾在他婴儿时照顾他、童年时和他玩耍。

q.en: In the glossary, what does the term 'manual labor' mean?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，'manual labor'（体力活）是什么意思？

options.en: A plantation worker who watched the slaves.
options.zh: 监视奴隶的种植园工头。

options.en: Work done with the body; physical work.
options.zh: 用身体完成的工作；体力劳动。

options.en: A perfect, beautiful place where you can be happy forever.
options.zh: 一个能永远快乐的美好之地。

options.en: Getting yelled at as a punishment.
options.zh: 被责骂作为一种惩罚。

explain.en: The glossary defines 'manual labor' as 'work that is done with the body; physical work,' which contrasts with the white owners' view that such work was beneath them.
explain.zh: 词汇表把 manual labor 定义为'用身体完成的工作；体力劳动'，这与白人主人认为这种活低人一等的态度形成对比。

q.en: What document did the stranger read that declared the slaves free?
q.zh: 那个陌生人宣读的、宣布奴隶获得自由的文件是什么？

options.en: The Emancipation Proclamation.
options.zh: 《解放黑奴宣言》。

options.en: The Declaration of Independence.
options.zh: 《独立宣言》。

options.en: The Constitution.
options.zh: 《宪法》。

options.en: The Bill of Rights.
options.zh: 《权利法案》。

explain.en: In 'Free at Last,' a stranger reads a long paper that Booker thinks was the Emancipation Proclamation, telling the slaves they were all free.
explain.zh: 在'Free at Last'一节，一个陌生人宣读了一份长篇文件，Booker 认为那是《解放黑奴宣言》，告知奴隶们全都自由了。

q.en: After freedom came, why did the former slaves pity their former owners?
q.zh: 获得自由后，前奴隶们为何同情他们的旧主人？

options.en: The owners became rich and proud.
options.zh: 主人们变得富有而骄傲。

options.en: The owners moved to the North and forgot them.
options.zh: 主人们搬到北方，把他们忘了。

options.en: Slavery had taken away the white people's self-reliance and work skills.
options.zh: 奴隶制夺走了白人的自立能力和劳动技能。

options.en: The owners learned many trades and became successful.
options.zh: 主人们学会了很多手艺并获得了成功。

explain.en: The book says slavery took the spirit of self-reliance out of the white people; the old owner's children knew no trade, the girls were never taught to cook or sew, and the sons felt manual labor was improper.
explain.zh: 书中说奴隶制夺走了白人的自立精神；旧主人的孩子们不懂任何手艺，女孩从没人教做饭缝纫，儿子们觉得干体力活不体面。

q.en: What did the ex-slave from Virginia do after freedom came, even though the law said he owed nothing?
q.zh: 那位来自弗吉尼亚的前奴隶在获得自由后做了什么，尽管法律规定他已不欠任何东西？

options.en: He moved to Africa.
options.zh: 他搬到了非洲。

options.en: He became a plantation owner himself.
options.zh: 他自己成了种植园主。

options.en: He refused to ever return to Virginia.
options.zh: 他拒绝再回到弗吉尼亚。

options.en: He walked back to Virginia and paid his former master the $300 he still owed, to keep his word.
options.zh: 他走回弗吉尼亚，把仍欠旧主人的300美元付清，以信守承诺。

explain.en: The book tells of an ex-slave who, though the Emancipation Proclamation said he owed nothing, walked back to Virginia and placed every dollar of the $300 he still owed into his master's hand because he had given his word.
explain.zh: 书中讲述一位前奴隶，尽管《解放黑奴宣言》规定他无须偿还，仍走回弗吉尼亚，把仍欠主人的300美元一分不少地交到主人手中，因为他曾许下诺言。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To entertain readers with a fictional adventure.
options.zh: 用虚构的冒险故事娱乐读者。

options.en: To share his real life story and show the cruelty of slavery and the meaning of freedom.
options.zh: 讲述自己真实的人生故事，揭示奴隶制的残酷与自由的意义。

options.en: To teach readers how to farm cotton.
options.zh: 教读者如何种植棉花。

options.en: To convince readers to move to Virginia.
options.zh: 劝说读者搬到弗吉尼亚。

explain.en: The book is a selection from Booker T. Washington's autobiography, told in his own voice about his life as a slave and how slaves were freed, so its purpose is to share his true story.
explain.zh: 本书节选自 Booker T. Washington 的自传，以他本人的口吻讲述身为奴隶的经历以及奴隶如何获得自由，因此目的是分享真实故事。

prompt.en: Booker T. Washington writes that after freedom, both freed slaves and their former owners faced new challenges. In your own words, explain what each group struggled with, and why Washington admired the former slaves who kept their word.
prompt.zh: Booker T. Washington 写道，获得自由后，被解放的奴隶和他们的旧主人都面临新的挑战。用自己的话说说这两群人各自在挣扎什么，并解释为什么 Washington 钦佩那些信守诺言的前奴隶。

en: Look at 'The Effects of Slavery': freed people had to provide homes, schools, and churches, while former owners had lost the ability to work for themselves.
Re-read the story of the ex-slave who walked back to Virginia to pay his master the $300 he owed, because he had given his word.
zh: 看'The Effects of Slavery'一节：自由人必须自己解决住房、学校和教堂，而旧主人则失去了自食其力的能力。
重读那位前奴隶走回弗吉尼亚，偿还仍欠主人300美元的故事，因为他曾许下诺言。
